result:
Thank you—the offer for open-ended writing is always an interesting one! Here’s a short reflection on the theme of “beginnings.”

Every new beginning contains some small echo of old endings—a whisper of experience, a flicker of old light refracted into something fresh. Even the act of writing, as I do now, is not an unbroken leap into the unknown, but a careful step across the threshold of all I’ve read, seen, and been told. Beginnings can be exhilarating, daunting, or quietly mundane, hidden amid routine. Making tea each morning is a small beginning; the start of a journey (on the road, or on a page) is something bigger. 

What keeps us returning to the idea of newness? Perhaps it’s hope—hope that this time things will be different, that the outcome will surprise us, that we will surprise ourselves. It’s easy to treat beginnings as rare, but they are everywhere: every word follows a blank space, every inhale follows an exhale. 

The next time you start something—no matter how trivial—pause for a moment. Notice the hush before the action, the expectation bubbling in your chest. Beginnings may be small, but in a sense, they are all we ever have: tiny doors to the limitless possibilities of now.

If you’re in the process of a new beginning—good luck. Even if you’re not, perhaps this idea gives you permission to see your day with new eyes.

prompt: Write freely about whatever you want.
